one_line_takeaway: PT-Mark makes latent-noise diffusion watermarks (Tree-Ring) far less destructive to image semantics by optimizing a per-step null-text steering vector to disentangle content from the watermark, keeping ~0.99 detection AUC while cutting FID from 42.97 to ~11 -- at the cost of a ~10x slower (paper's figure; table implies ~13x), iteration-count-sensitive embedding.
key_finding: On DiffusionDB, PT-Mark reaches PSNR 28.18, SSIM 0.94, MSSIM 0.97, FID 11.32, LPIPS 0.03 with average detection AUC 0.99, versus Tree-Ring's PSNR 15.18 / SSIM 0.56 / FID 42.97 at the same 0.99 AUC -- i.e., it recovers most of the image quality Tree-Ring sacrifices while matching its robustness. On MS-COCO it reaches PSNR 27.38 / SSIM 0.90 / FID 7.96 / LPIPS 0.04 at avg AUC 0.98. Efficiency: 149.94s inference vs. Zodiac's 684.67s (~4x faster) and ROBIN's 1374.21s total (ROBIN needs 1370s per-user training); but ~13x slower than Tree-Ring's 11.65s. The ablation shows the watermark-preservation objective is what buys robustness: semantic-tuning-only (w/o WP) gives slightly better quality (PSNR 28.33, FID 10.73) but average robustness collapses to 0.87 (noise 0.77, rotation 0.80), which the full method restores to 0.99. The abstract's headline '10% improvement in semantic preservation' and '4x greater efficiency' are supported by these tables.
core_contribution: PT-Mark (Semantic-aware Pivotal Tuning Watermark) is an invisible watermarking method for text-to-image diffusion models that aims to keep a traceable latent-space watermark while restoring the semantic fidelity that existing latent watermarks (e.g., Tree-Ring) destroy. The key idea is to edit the ENTIRE diffusion denoising trajectory, not just the initial noise: after embedding a Tree-Ring-style watermark into the initial latent, PT-Mark optimizes a per-step learnable null-text embedding (a steering vector) so the generation trajectory realigns with the original (pivotal) DDIM-inverted trajectory in watermark-agnostic regions while staying aligned with the watermarked trajectory inside watermark-salient regions. A pretrained SAM segmentation network computes the salient watermark mask at each timestep to disentangle semantics from the watermark pattern. It is training-free on the diffusion model and presented as a plug-and-play module that can be dropped onto existing latent-noise watermarking methods using the same DDIM-inversion + Fourier verification pipeline. NOTE: the null-text/pivotal-tuning mechanism itself (Null-text Inversion, Mokady et al. [21]) is CITED prior work; PT-Mark's own novelty is repurposing it for watermarking via the two-objective (semantic vs. watermark) disentanglement loss and SAM-derived salient-region guidance.
threat_model: Copyright / IP-protection and provenance setting for AI-generated images. The defender (model owner or content creator) embeds an invisible, user-specific watermark to later prove ownership of a generated image. The adversary is modeled implicitly as (a) real-world channels that degrade images (JPEG compression, cropping, blur, Gaussian noise, brightness/color jitter, rotation) as when images are shared on social networks, and (b) briefly, adaptive watermark-removal attacks (referenced to an appendix, which is a broken '??' cross-reference in this version). The watermark must remain detectable (verified via DDIM inversion, Fourier-domain statistical p-value / non-central chi-squared test, scored by AUC) under these perturbations while remaining visually invisible.
limitations: Robustness and invisibility trade off against optimization iterations: at 20 iterations quality peaks but brightness-attack AUC drops to 0.46 (overfitting to the clean distribution), so the sweet spot (10 iters) is empirically tuned, not principled. Inference is ~10x slower (paper's stated figure; the timing table's 149.94s vs 11.65s implies ~13x) than the Tree-Ring baseline it improves on, which is heavy for high-throughput deployment despite the 4x edge over Zodiac. The method depends on external components (SAM segmentation and a Tree-Ring-style latent watermark backbone) and is only demonstrated with the Tree-Ring pattern. Inference is ~10x slower than Tree-Ring by the paper's own text (line ~1013), though the timing table (149.94s vs 11.65s) implies closer to ~13x. The adaptive-removal-attack results that would most stress the security claim are deferred to an appendix that is a broken cross-reference ('Appendix ??') in this arXiv version, so cannot be assessed here. Core tuning mechanism is inherited from Null-text Inversion rather than novel.

datasets_used: MS-COCO (5000 validation captions used as generation prompts)
quantitative_results: PT-Mark on DiffusionDB: PSNR 28.18, SSIM 0.94, MSSIM 0.97, FID 11.32, LPIPS 0.03, avg detection AUC 0.99
PT-Mark on MS-COCO: PSNR 27.38, SSIM 0.90, MSSIM 0.97, FID 7.96, LPIPS 0.04, avg detection AUC 0.98
Tree-Ring on DiffusionDB: PSNR 15.18, SSIM 0.56, MSSIM 0.59, FID 42.97, LPIPS 0.37, avg AUC 0.99 (quality baseline PT-Mark improves on)
Efficiency (Table 2): PT-Mark inference 149.94s (0 training) vs Zodiac 684.67s (~4x faster), Tree-Ring 11.65s, ROBIN 1370.48s train + 3.74s infer = 1374.21s total
Ablation: semantic-tuning only (w/o watermark-preservation) = PSNR 28.33, SSIM 0.94, FID 10.73 but avg robustness 0.87 (noise 0.77, rotation 0.80); full PT-Mark restores avg robustness to 0.99
Iteration ablation: at 20 iterations, brightness-attack AUC falls to 0.46 (vs 1.00 at 10 iterations); FID improves 12.28 -> 10.65 with more iterations; step=10 chosen as best trade-off
Perturbation suite: JPEG (QF 25), crop (75%), Gaussian blur (radius 4), Gaussian noise (10%), brightness jitter (factor 6), rotation (up to 75 deg); rotation and noise most damaging to baselines
baselines_compared: Tree-Ring (latent-noise, in-generation; also PT-Mark's watermark backbone)
